steps.title: Connect the condition to the discriminant
steps.content:
The question asks when the quadratic has more than one real solution. For a quadratic, that happens exactly when the discriminant is positive:
$$b^2 - 4ac \gt 0$$
In $77x^2 + bx + c = 0$, the coefficient of $x^2$ is $77$, so:
$$b^2 - 4(77)c \gt 0$$
$$b^2 - 308c \gt 0$$
$$b^2 \gt 308c$$

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content: Since $b$ is positive, take the square root: $b \gt \sqrt{308c}$. That is the correct answer.
